input.prompt:
I updated to a newer .NET SDK on Windows 10 and now my .NET MAUI app
fails to build with workload and Android SDK errors. A colleague told me
to run 'dotnet workload update' but I'm not sure that's safe. Please lay
out a diagnostic and fix plan that brings my workloads and Android SDK
into a consistent state for the new SDK.

input.initialPrompt:
I updated to a newer .NET SDK on Windows 10 and now my .NET MAUI app
fails to build with workload and Android SDK errors. A colleague told me
to run 'dotnet workload update' but I'm not sure that's safe. Please lay
out a diagnostic and fix plan that brings my workloads and Android SDK
into a consistent state for the new SDK.

data.content:
Here's a step-by-step plan to get back to a clean state:

## 1 — Diagnose

```powershell
# Record what you're working with
dotnet --info
dotnet workload list
```

Note the SDK version (e.g. `9.0.200`) and which workloads are installed. Mismatched workload manifest versions are the most common cause.

## 2 — Repair workloads

`dotnet workload update` **is safe** — it pulls manifests matching your SDK band and re-installs packs. Run it from an **elevated** (Administrator) terminal:

```powershell
dotnet workload update
```

If you see errors about missing feeds, make sure you have the default NuGet source:

```powershell
dotnet nuget list source
# If missing: dotnet nuget add source https://api.nuget.org/v3/index.json -n nuget.org
```

## 3 — Verify required workloads

MAUI needs at minimum the `maui` workload (which pulls `android`, `ios`, etc.):

```powershell
dotnet workload install maui
```

This is idempotent — it won't break anything if already installed.

## 4 — Fix Android SDK

The Android workload installs its own SDK under `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Microsoft\Android\sdk`. Common errors:

| Error | Fix |
|---|---|
| *"Android SDK not found"* | Set `ANDROID_HOME` → `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Microsoft\Android\sdk` |
| *"platform/build-tools missing"* | Accept licenses & install components ↓ |

```powershell
# Accept all licenses (required after SDK updates)
& "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses

# Ensure required components are present (adjust API level to your TFM)
& "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
    "platforms;android-35" "build-tools;35.0.0" "platform-tools"
```

## 5 — Clean and rebuild

```powershell
# From your solution directory
dotnet clean
# Delete bin/obj to remove stale restore artifacts
Remove-Item -Recurse -Force *\bin, *\obj
dotnet build
```

## 6 — If still broken — nuclear option

```powershell
# Remove all workloads, then reinstall cleanly
dotnet workload uninstall maui
dotnet workload install maui
```

## Key takeaway

`dotnet workload update` is the correct first move — it aligns manifests to your new SDK band. The remaining steps handle the Android SDK licensing/components that the workload updater doesn't always resolve automatically.
